The description currently lives only in the theorem's docstring. Adding a sentence like *"Every connected graph on n vertices can be partitioned into at most ⌈n/2⌉ edge-disjoint paths"* to the module docstring would satisfy this requirement.
    92→
    93→**Everything else conforms.** The formalization is clean and mathematically sound. The edge-partition into paths is correctly captured: the statement requires the existence of at most ⌈n/2⌉ paths (via `k ≤ (n + 1) / 2` in natural number division) that are vertex-simple (`IsPath`), pairwise edge-disjoint, and collectively cover all edges of G. The `haveI := dG` in the theorem type correctly brings the `DecidableRel` instance into scope for `G.edgeFinset`. The Case 3 (direct assertion) treatment is appropriate for this conjecture.

file.content:
# Review: deepmind/583.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# Erdős Problem 583`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source: `[erdosproblems.com/583](https://www.erdosproblems.com/583)`.
- [ ] **FAIL.** The module docstring does not include a brief plain-English description of the problem. The problem description ("Every connected graph on $n$ vertices can be partitioned into at most $\lceil n/2 \rceil$ edge-disjoint paths") appears only in the theorem's docstring, not in the module docstring. The checklist requires the module docstring itself to contain a brief description.
- [x] Bibliography entry present: `[ErGa59]` for Erdős and Gallai, 1959.

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos583 ... end Erdos583`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos583`.
- [x] `open SimpleGraph` appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

N/A — no `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations in this file.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_583` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` conventions.
- [x] `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the same attribute line: `@[category research open, AMS 5]`.
- [x] Has a docstring (`/-- ... -/`) explaining the conjecture.
- [x] Docstring references source paper: `[ErGa59]`.

### `answer()` usage

The original problem states: *"Every connected graph on $n$ vertices can be partitioned into at most $\lceil n/2 \rceil$ edge-disjoint paths."* This is a direct assertion (Case 3 — the problem asserts a fact, not a yes/no question or a find-the-value question).

- [x] The theorem type is `P` directly (no `answer()` wrapper), which is correct for Case 3.
- [x] N/A — quantifier-placement rule only applies when `answer()` is used. Having `n`, `G`, `dG`, `hconn` as theorem parameters is standard Lean style for universally quantified Case 3 statements.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` present.
- [x] Value `research open` is valid (open research problem).
- [x] Proof body contains `sorry`, consistent with `research open`.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute: `AMS 5`.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed (`5` — Combinatorics).
- [x] Numbers in ascending order (only one number).
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code.

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Uses `by\n  sorry` (multi-line format, acceptable per checklist).
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions (no definitions present).
- [x] No informal proof sketches for the open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

N/A — no variants present. The file formalizes only the core conjecture.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout (continuation lines use standard Lean formatting for multi-line signatures).
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `ℕ`, `∀`, `∃`, `≤`, `∧`, `↔`, `∈`, `≠`, `Σ`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] Not verified in this review (requires `lake build`).

---

## Summary

**One conformance issue found:**

1. **Missing plain-English description in module docstring (§3).** The module docstring contains the heading, reference link, and bibliography entry, but lacks a brief plain-English description of the problem. The description currently lives only in the theorem's docstring. Adding a sentence like *"Every connected graph on n vertices can be partitioned into at most ⌈n/2⌉ edge-disjoint paths"* to the module docstring would satisfy this requirement.

**Everything else conforms.** The formalization is clean and mathematically sound. The edge-partition into paths is correctly captured: the statement requires the existence of at most ⌈n/2⌉ paths (via `k ≤ (n + 1) / 2` in natural number division) that are vertex-simple (`IsPath`), pairwise edge-disjoint, and collectively cover all edges of G. The `haveI := dG` in the theorem type correctly brings the `DecidableRel` instance into scope for `G.edgeFinset`. The Case 3 (direct assertion) treatment is appropriate for this conjecture.

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erdős Problem 583

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/583](https://www.erdosproblems.com/583)

[ErGa59] Erdős, P. and Gallai, T., *On the minimal number of vertices representing the
edges of a graph*, 1959.
-/

open SimpleGraph

namespace Erdos583

/--
Erdős Problem 583 [ErGa59]:

Every connected graph on $n$ vertices can be partitioned into at most $\lceil n/2 \rceil$
edge-disjoint paths.
-/
@[category research open, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_583 (n : ℕ) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n))
    (dG : DecidableRel G.Adj) (hconn : G.Connected) :
    haveI := dG
    ∃ (k : ℕ) (paths : Fin k → Σ (v w : Fin n), G.Walk v w),
      k ≤ (n + 1) / 2 ∧
      (∀ i, (paths i).2.2.IsPath) ∧
      (∀ i j : Fin k, i ≠ j →
        Disjoint (paths i).2.2.edges.toFinset (paths j).2.2.edges.toFinset) ∧
      (∀ e, e ∈ G.edgeFinset ↔ ∃ i, e ∈ (paths i).2.2.edges.toFinset) := by
  sorry

end Erdos583

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# Erdős Problem 583

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/583](https://www.erdosproblems.com/583)

[ErGa59] Erdős, P. and Gallai, T., *On the minimal number of vertices representing the
edges of a graph*, 1959.
-/

open SimpleGraph

namespace Erdos583

/--
Erdős Problem 583 [ErGa59]:

Every connected graph on $n$ vertices can be partitioned into at most $\lceil n/2 \rceil$
edge-disjoint paths.
-/
@[category research open, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_583 (n : ℕ) (G : SimpleGraph (Fin n))
    (dG : DecidableRel G.Adj) (hconn : G.Connected) :
    haveI := dG
    ∃ (k : ℕ) (paths : Fin k → Σ (v w : Fin n), G.Walk v w),
      k ≤ (n + 1) / 2 ∧
      (∀ i, (paths i).2.2.IsPath) ∧
      (∀ i j : Fin k, i ≠ j →
        Disjoint (paths i).2.2.edges.toFinset (paths j).2.2.edges.toFinset) ∧
      (∀ e, e ∈ G.edgeFinset ↔ ∃ i, e ∈ (paths i).2.2.edges.toFinset) := by
  sorry

end Erdos583
